Arabidopsis thaliana, also known as Olimarabidopsis pumila in some classifications, is a small flowering plant belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae). 

The health benefits of Arabidopsis thaliana are largely unexplored, but its bioactive constituents include glucosinolates, which have been shown to have potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Arabidopsis thaliana include infusions and decoctions, although they are not widely used in traditional medicine due to the lack of research on its health benefits.
